title: Add M4 benchmarks and invariants
summary: Add M4 long-run benchmark and invariant gates for lamp network, Chronicle evidence, obligations, crisis chains, director recovery windows, save/replay, Worker parity and M0-M3 regression protection.
acceptance: M4 invariant gate runs the core vertical slice long enough to detect lamp dirty backlog growth evidence drift obligation leaks invalid crisis transitions director recovery-window violations save/replay divergence Worker parity mismatch and M0-M3 regression

Benchmarks report lamp dirty counts active gaps evidence support candidates confirmed rules dissemination backlog obligation due and violation counts town-rule compliance candidates crisis transition counts director candidates recovery windows ReasonTrace capacity save/load rebuild time Worker projection bytes and final hashes
Benchmark artifacts include Node pnpm OS CPU commit seed scenario id tick horizon checkpoint hashes final summary and reviewed SHA-256
Baseline updates are reviewed artifacts and preserve 10 percent warning and 20 percent blocking thresholds
No product behavior fix threshold weakening silent retry repeatEach retries public protocol save/schema dependency UI authority or M5 work is included

history.details: Created by WM-0061 M4 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.
Added M4 long-run invariants and default benchmark artifact with 100000-tick samples, save/replay, Worker parity, baseline, docs and report; required checks pass.

changes_requested: High: WM-0070 benchmark artifact hash evidence is not reproducible. coordination/reports/WM-0070.md and the artifact reviewedSha256 record 2E537A601FE41868C02E89D57322318F88841F471DF20749638FC17FF0674DE3, but Get-FileHash on coordination/artifacts/WM-0070/benchmarks/benchmark-results.json returns BE28064528C2144DC1A2186783ADF83D4607DB723AC92DCAADFF0D0AB414239D. The benchmark writer computes reviewedSha256 before adding that field, so the published hash is not the actual artifact file hash. Fix the hash convention/evidence so the reviewed artifact path and hash are exact, reproducible, and unambiguous.

Repaired WM-0070 benchmark artifact hash convention: renamed pre-field digest to canonicalPayloadSha256, added actual benchmark-results.json SHA-256 sidecar, regenerated artifact/docs/report, and reran required typecheck, m4 invariants, bench, sim, validate, diff check, plus quality.

verified: No blocking findings. Re-review confirms the prior artifact hash issue is fixed: benchmark-results.json SHA-256 FDC0DFE779264134C23F3DAF95C92C879C683DDCBB22A9E704F12359B3DC0E71 matches the .sha256 sidecar and report, while canonicalPayloadSha256 is clearly labeled as the pre-hashing payload digest. Baseline thresholds remain 10/20, scope stays within WM-0070 benchmark/invariant/docs/artifact paths, and required gates pass.
Verified by reviewer; quality, m4 invariants, temp-artifact bench, 100000-tick sim, validate and diff gates passed

objective: Create artifact-backed M4 performance and invariant evidence before closeout while protecting all prior milestone gates.
forbiddenPaths: product behavior fixes to satisfy tests
silent retries repeatEach or retries changes
benchmarkImpact: Adds reviewed M4 long-run benchmark artifacts and baseline changes only with explicit 10/20 percent threshold preservation and M0-M3 regression evidence.
reviewRouting: Qa-performance completes to reviewer; project-director integrates only after artifact evidence and required checks are reproducible.
rollbackModel: Revert M4 benchmark tests artifacts and baseline changes; leave M4 open and do not advance closeout.
ownerGate: Name exact artifact paths and hashes in the report
Do not fix product behavior inside the benchmark task
Block on unreproducible artifacts threshold weakening protocol save schema dependency UI authority or M5 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Benchmark baselines and long-run invariant gates require qa-performance ownership and independent review.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Re-review confirms the prior artifact hash issue is fixed: benchmark-results.json SHA-256 FDC0DFE779264134C23F3DAF95C92C879C683DDCBB22A9E704F12359B3DC0E71 matches the .sha256 sidecar and report, while canonicalPayloadSha256 is clearly labeled as the pre-hashing payload digest. Baseline thresholds remain 10/20, scope stays within WM-0070 benchmark/invariant/docs/artifact paths, and required gates pass.

integration.summary: Verified by reviewer; quality, m4 invariants, temp-artifact bench, 100000-tick sim, validate and diff gates passed
